name: ATI CMS Peds Practice A — Comprehensive

questions.question: A nurse is caring for a 6-month-old infant at a well-child visit. The parent asks how to promote healthy development. Based on Erikson's psychosocial theory, which nursing intervention best supports the infant's developmental task?
options: Provide consistent, responsive care and meet needs promptly
Offer choices to promote independence
Encourage fantasy play with role assignments
Support achievement of academic tasks
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Provide consistent, responsive care and meet needs promptly' — because Trust vs Mistrust (0-1 year) requires consistent, responsive caregiving. Promptly meeting needs (feeding, comfort, diaper changes) builds trust. INCORRECT: 'Offer choices to promote independence' — because Option B (Autonomy vs Shame) is for toddlers 1-3 years. INCORRECT: 'Encourage fantasy play with role assignments' — because Option C (Initiative vs Guilt) is for preschoolers 3-6 years. INCORRECT: 'Support achievement of academic tasks' — because Option D (Industry vs Inferiority) is for school-age 6-12 years.

questions.question: A nurse is assessing a 9-month-old infant. Which developmental milestones are expected at this age? (Select all that apply.)
options: Pincer grasp with thumb and index finger
Independent walking without assistance
Stranger anxiety and separation distress
Sitting unsupported and crawling
Saying 'mama' and 'dada' with meaning
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Pincer grasp with thumb and index finger' — because 9-month milestones include pincer grasp (fine motor). CORRECT: 'Stranger anxiety and separation distress' — because stranger anxiety (social/emotional). CORRECT: 'Sitting unsupported and crawling' — because sitting unsupported and crawling (gross motor). CORRECT: 'Saying 'mama' and 'dada' with meaning' — because meaningful 'mama/dada' (language). INCORRECT: 'Independent walking without assistance' — because independent walking is 12-15 months.

questions.question: A parent of a 10-month-old infant asks about car seat safety. Which teaching by the nurse is correct?
options: Rear-facing car seat in the back seat until the infant is at least 1 year and 20 pounds
Forward-facing car seat is appropriate once the infant can sit alone
The infant can ride in the front seat if the airbag is disabled
Booster seat is appropriate starting at 12 months
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Rear-facing car seat in the back seat until the infant is at least 1 year and 20 pounds' — because AAP guidelines and ATI safety teaching recommend rear-facing car seats in the back seat until age 2 or manufacturer max height/weight (whichever comes last). INCORRECT: 'Forward-facing car seat is appropriate once the infant can sit alone' — because forward-facing too early increases injury risk. INCORRECT: 'The infant can ride in the front seat if the airbag is disabled' — because front seats are never recommended for infants. INCORRECT: 'Booster seat is appropriate starting at 12 months' — because booster is for older children.

questions.question: A nurse is providing anticipatory guidance to parents of a 2-year-old. Which safety measures should be included? (Select all that apply.)
options: Turn pot handles to the back of the stove
Keep all medications and cleaning supplies locked
Allow the child to sleep in a car seat at home for convenience
Use a forward-facing car seat after the child turns 1 year
Supervise near water and empty pools/bathtubs immediately
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Turn pot handles to the back of the stove' — because pot handles back (burn prevention). CORRECT: 'Keep all medications and cleaning supplies locked' — because locked cabinets (poisoning prevention). CORRECT: 'Supervise near water and empty pools/bathtubs immediately' — because water supervision (drowning prevention — leading cause of death in toddlers). INCORRECT: 'Allow the child to sleep in a car seat at home for convenience' — because car seats are for vehicles, not sleeping (risk of positional asphyxia). INCORRECT: 'Use a forward-facing car seat after the child turns 1 year' — because AAP recommends rear-facing until age 2.

questions.question: A 10-month-old infant is observed searching for a toy hidden under a cloth. This behavior demonstrates which Piaget cognitive concept?
options: Object permanence
Egocentrism
Conservation
Abstract reasoning
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Object permanence' — because object permanence develops around 8-10 months in the sensorimotor stage. The infant understands the toy still exists when hidden. INCORRECT: 'Egocentrism' — because egocentrism is preoperational (2-7 years). INCORRECT: 'Conservation' — because conservation is concrete operational (7-11 years). INCORRECT: 'Abstract reasoning' — because abstract reasoning is formal operational (11+ years).

questions.question: Match each family description with the correct classification:
rows: Biological parents and their children living together
A parent remarries, bringing children from previous relationships
A single parent raising children alone
Grandparents living with parents and children
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Biological parents and their children living together' matches 'Nuclear family' — because nuclear family. CORRECT: 'A parent remarries, bringing children from previous relationships' matches 'Blended family' — because blended family. CORRECT: 'A single parent raising children alone' matches 'Single-parent family' — because single-parent family. CORRECT: 'Grandparents living with parents and children' matches 'Extended family' — because extended family.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching new parents about SIDS prevention. Which statement indicates understanding?
options: We will place our baby on her back to sleep and use a firm mattress
We will place soft bedding and pillows in the crib for comfort
We will co-sleep with the baby in our bed for bonding
We will place the baby on her stomach when she has a cold
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'We will place our baby on her back to sleep and use a firm mattress' — because back to sleep on a firm mattress with no soft bedding is the AAP Safe Sleep recommendation to reduce SIDS risk. INCORRECT: 'We will place soft bedding and pillows in the crib for comfort' — because increases suffocation risk. INCORRECT: 'We will co-sleep with the baby in our bed for bonding' — because co-sleeping increases SIDS risk per AAP. INCORRECT: 'We will place the baby on her stomach when she has a cold' — because prone position increases SIDS.

questions.question: A nurse is assessing an infant for dehydration. Which findings indicate moderate dehydration? (Select all that apply.)
options: Sunken fontanelle and dry mucous membranes
Capillary refill greater than 3 seconds
Increased urine output
Tachycardia and irritability
Skin turgor that returns slowly
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Sunken fontanelle and dry mucous membranes' — because sunken fontanelle, dry mucous membranes. CORRECT: 'Capillary refill greater than 3 seconds' — because delayed capillary refill. CORRECT: 'Tachycardia and irritability' — because tachycardia, irritability. CORRECT: 'Skin turgor that returns slowly' — because slow skin turgor. INCORRECT: 'Increased urine output' — because decreased urine output is expected.

questions.question: A parent reports that their 3-year-old pulled a pot of boiling water off the stove, sustaining burns to the face and chest. Which teaching should the nurse provide to prevent future burns?
options: Keep pot handles turned to the back of the stove and cook on back burners
Allow the child to help cook to promote autonomy
Keep hot beverages on low tables within reach
Test bath water with the child's hand before bathing
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Keep pot handles turned to the back of the stove and cook on back burners' — because pot handles to the back and using back burners prevent toddlers from grabbing hot items. INCORRECT: 'Allow the child to help cook to promote autonomy' — because young children should not help cook near hot surfaces. INCORRECT: 'Keep hot beverages on low tables within reach' — because hot liquids within reach cause burns. INCORRECT: 'Test bath water with the child's hand before bathing' — because use a thermometer or wrist/elbow to test.

questions.question: A nurse is providing oral health teaching for parents of an 18-month-old. Which statement is correct?
options: Brush the child's teeth twice daily with a soft brush and fluoridated toothpaste (pea-sized amount)
Avoid fluoride until age 6 years to prevent fluorosis
Give a bottle of juice at bedtime for comfort
Start brushing at age 3 years when the child can cooperate
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Brush the child's teeth twice daily with a soft brush and fluoridated toothpaste (pea-sized amount)' — because brush twice daily with soft brush and pea-sized fluoridated toothpaste starting with first tooth. INCORRECT: 'Avoid fluoride until age 6 years to prevent fluorosis' — because fluoride is recommended starting at 6 months. INCORRECT: 'Give a bottle of juice at bedtime for comfort' — because causes nursing bottle caries. INCORRECT: 'Start brushing at age 3 years when the child can cooperate' — because brushing should start with first tooth, not delayed.

questions.question: A nurse receives report on four pediatric clients. Which client should the nurse see first?
options: A 6-month-old with nasal flaring, grunting, and oxygen saturation of 88%
A 3-year-old with a fever of 38.5°C and a mild cough
A 7-year-old with a history of asthma who is playing quietly
A 12-year-old with a sprained ankle requesting pain medication
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'A 6-month-old with nasal flaring, grunting, and oxygen saturation of 88%' — because ABCs — this infant has respiratory distress (nasal flaring, grunting, SpO2 88%). This is an emergency requiring immediate intervention. INCORRECT: 'A 3-year-old with a fever of 38.5°C and a mild cough' — because stable fever, mild cough is lower priority. INCORRECT: 'A 7-year-old with a history of asthma who is playing quietly' — because stable asthma. INCORRECT: 'A 12-year-old with a sprained ankle requesting pain medication' — because non-urgent.

questions.question: A nurse is assessing a 2-year-old with gastroenteritis for dehydration. Which findings require immediate intervention? (Select all that apply.)
options: Sunken eyes and fontanelle
Capillary refill of 4 seconds
Moist mucous membranes
Absence of tears when crying
Alert and playful behavior
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Sunken eyes and fontanelle' — because sunken eyes/fontanelle. CORRECT: 'Capillary refill of 4 seconds' — because delayed capillary refill (>3 sec). CORRECT: 'Absence of tears when crying' — because absence of tears. INCORRECT: 'Moist mucous membranes' — because moist membranes indicates adequate hydration. INCORRECT: 'Alert and playful behavior' — because alert/playful indicates adequate hydration.

questions.question: A 4-year-old child is crying and pointing to their knee after a fall. Which pain assessment tool is most appropriate for this age?
options: FACES Pain Scale
Numeric 0–10 Scale
FLACC Behavioral Scale
Oucher Scale (photographic)
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'FACES Pain Scale' — because FACES is appropriate for children 3+ years who can point to a face matching their pain. INCORRECT: 'Numeric 0–10 Scale' — because numeric requires abstract thinking (best for 8+ years). INCORRECT: 'FLACC Behavioral Scale' — because FLACC is for nonverbal/infants. INCORRECT: 'Oucher Scale (photographic)' — because Oucher is also valid for 3+ but FACES is more commonly used per ATI.

questions.question: Match each pain assessment tool with the correct age group:
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'FACES Scale' matches 'Preschool/School-age (3+ years)' — because FACES (3+ years, can point to faces). CORRECT: 'FLACC Scale' matches 'Infants/Toddlers (nonverbal)' — because FLACC (nonverbal, behavioral cues). CORRECT: 'Numeric 0–10 Scale' matches 'School-age/Adolescents (8+ years)' — because Numeric (abstract thinking required). CORRECT: 'CRIES Scale' matches 'Infants (0–6 months)' — because CRIES (infants, Crying/Requires O2/Increased vitals/Expression/Sleeplessness).

questions.question: In the pediatric ED, a nurse is triaging using the Pediatric Assessment Triangle. Which child requires emergent (immediate) care?
options: An infant with poor muscle tone, grunting respirations, and pale skin
A toddler with a rash and mild fever, playing with toys
A school-age child with a laceration on the arm, stable vitals
An adolescent with a sprained wrist, walking independently
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'An infant with poor muscle tone, grunting respirations, and pale skin' — because poor tone, abnormal breathing (grunting), and abnormal circulation (pallor) = all three PAT components abnormal → emergent care. INCORRECT: 'A toddler with a rash and mild fever, playing with toys' — because stable. INCORRECT: 'A school-age child with a laceration on the arm, stable vitals' — because urgent but not emergent. INCORRECT: 'An adolescent with a sprained wrist, walking independently' — because minor injury.

questions.question: A nurse is preparing a 6-year-old for a painful dressing change. Which interventions should be included? (Select all that apply.)
options: Administer prescribed analgesic 30–60 minutes before the procedure
Allow the child to hold a comfort object during the procedure
Explain the procedure in detail including all technical terms
Use distraction techniques (bubbles, music, video)
Perform the dressing change without warning the child
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer prescribed analgesic 30–60 minutes before the procedure' — because premedicate (30–60 min before). CORRECT: 'Allow the child to hold a comfort object during the procedure' — because allow comfort objects. CORRECT: 'Use distraction techniques (bubbles, music, video)' — because use distraction. INCORRECT: 'Explain the procedure in detail including all technical terms' — because use simple, concrete terms for this age. INCORRECT: 'Perform the dressing change without warning the child' — because unethical and increases fear.

questions.question: A nurse is prioritizing care for a hospitalized 5-year-old. Using Maslow's hierarchy, which need should be addressed first?
options: Maintaining adequate hydration and nutrition
Providing age-appropriate toys for play
Allowing the child to call parents on video chat
Praising the child for completing a puzzle
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Maintaining adequate hydration and nutrition' — because physiological needs (hydration, nutrition) are the foundation of Maslow's hierarchy and must be met first. INCORRECT: 'Providing age-appropriate toys for play' — because play is belonging/esteem. INCORRECT: 'Allowing the child to call parents on video chat' — because video call is belonging. INCORRECT: 'Praising the child for completing a puzzle' — because praise is esteem.

questions.question: A nurse is preparing a 4-year-old for surgery. Which interventions reduce preoperative anxiety? (Select all that apply.)
options: Allow the child to play with medical equipment (mask, stethoscope)
Provide simple, honest explanations of what to expect
Separate the child from parents during preparation
Use therapeutic play (dolls, drawings) to explain the procedure
Promise the child there will be no pain
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Allow the child to play with medical equipment (mask, stethoscope)' — because medical play. CORRECT: 'Provide simple, honest explanations of what to expect' — because simple explanations. CORRECT: 'Use therapeutic play (dolls, drawings) to explain the procedure' — because therapeutic play. INCORRECT: 'Separate the child from parents during preparation' — because parental presence reduces separation anxiety. INCORRECT: 'Promise the child there will be no pain' — because false reassurance — some pain is expected.

questions.question: A 3-year-old with vomiting and diarrhea has had 4 wet diapers in 24 hours. The parent is concerned. Which response by the nurse is correct?
options: Four wet diapers per day is adequate hydration for a 3-year-old
The child needs at least 8 wet diapers per day to be considered hydrated
We should start IV fluids immediately
The child is severely dehydrated and needs hospitalization
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Four wet diapers per day is adequate hydration for a 3-year-old' — because 4-6 wet diapers per day is adequate for a toddler. Infants should have 6-8, but toddlers have larger bladders. INCORRECT: 'The child needs at least 8 wet diapers per day to be considered hydrated' — because for infants. INCORRECT: 'We should start IV fluids immediately' — because not indicated — child is not severely dehydrated. INCORRECT: 'The child is severely dehydrated and needs hospitalization' — because not severely dehydrated (wet diapers, alert).

questions.question: A nurse is planning care for a 5-year-old scheduled for a dressing change. Which statement reflects best practice for pain management?
options: Administer pain medication 30–60 minutes before the dressing change
Wait until the child reports pain before giving medication
Use distraction alone without medication for painful procedures
Avoid opioids in children due to respiratory depression risk
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer pain medication 30–60 minutes before the dressing change' — because premedication 30-60 minutes before painful procedures prevents pain escalation and reduces anticipatory anxiety. INCORRECT: 'Wait until the child reports pain before giving medication' — because allows pain to escalate. INCORRECT: 'Use distraction alone without medication for painful procedures' — because insufficient for moderate-severe pain. INCORRECT: 'Avoid opioids in children due to respiratory depression risk' — because opioids are appropriate for severe pain with monitoring.

questions.question: A school-age child with asthma is using a peak flow meter. Which reading indicates the child should use quick-relief medication and may need to call the provider?
options: Yellow zone (50–79% of personal best)
Green zone (80–100% of personal best)
Red zone (below 50% of personal best)
Any reading is acceptable if the child feels fine
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Yellow zone (50–79% of personal best)' — because yellow zone (50-79%) means caution — use quick-relief inhaler, follow action plan, may limit activity. INCORRECT: 'Green zone (80–100% of personal best)' — because green zone is good control. INCORRECT: 'Red zone (below 50% of personal best)' — because red zone is emergency (below 50%) — seek immediate care. INCORRECT: 'Any reading is acceptable if the child feels fine' — because not acceptable.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching parents about home care for a child with cystic fibrosis. Which interventions should be included? (Select all that apply.)
options: Perform chest physiotherapy twice daily
Give pancreatic enzymes with every meal and snack
Restrict fluids to reduce mucus production
Provide a high-calorie, high-protein diet
Administer fat-soluble vitamin supplements (A, D, E, K)
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Perform chest physiotherapy twice daily' — because CPT clears mucus. CORRECT: 'Give pancreatic enzymes with every meal and snack' — because enzymes with meals aid digestion (CF blocks pancreatic ducts). CORRECT: 'Provide a high-calorie, high-protein diet' — because high-calorie/protein combats malabsorption. CORRECT: 'Administer fat-soluble vitamin supplements (A, D, E, K)' — because ADEK vitamins replace fat-soluble vitamins not absorbed. INCORRECT: 'Restrict fluids to reduce mucus production' — because hydration is essential to thin mucus.

questions.question: A 2-year-old presents with a barking cough, stridor, and mild retractions at rest. Which intervention is most appropriate?
options: Administer nebulized racemic epinephrine and oral corticosteroids
Prepare for immediate intubation
Perform a throat examination to assess epiglottis
Discharge home with no treatment
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer nebulized racemic epinephrine and oral corticosteroids' — because moderate croup (stridor at rest, retractions) is treated with nebulized racemic epinephrine (reduces airway swelling) and corticosteroids (dexamethasone reduces inflammation). INCORRECT: 'Prepare for immediate intubation' — because for severe respiratory failure. INCORRECT: 'Perform a throat examination to assess epiglottis' — because contraindicated — may trigger epiglottitis. INCORRECT: 'Discharge home with no treatment' — because unsafe.

questions.question: A 4-month-old with bronchiolitis (RSV) is admitted. Which intervention is the priority?
options: Suction nasal passages before feeding and monitor oxygen saturation
Administer antibiotics immediately
Place in a mist tent with cool humidified air
Isolate from other infants on the unit
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Suction nasal passages before feeding and monitor oxygen saturation' — because RSV causes nasal congestion blocking breathing — suctioning before feeds ensures adequate nutrition/hydration. Monitor SpO2 for hypoxia. INCORRECT: 'Administer antibiotics immediately' — because RSV is viral. INCORRECT: 'Place in a mist tent with cool humidified air' — because outdated. INCORRECT: 'Isolate from other infants on the unit' — because contact precautions, but priority is airway.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching a family about asthma trigger avoidance. Which factors should be avoided? (Select all that apply.)
options: Secondhand smoke and pet dander
Cold air and respiratory infections
Daily physical exercise
Dust mites and mold
Inhaled corticosteroids
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Secondhand smoke and pet dander' — because smoke, pet dander. CORRECT: 'Cold air and respiratory infections' — because cold air, infections. CORRECT: 'Dust mites and mold' — because dust mites, mold. INCORRECT: 'Daily physical exercise' — because exercise is beneficial (pre-treat with albuterol). INCORRECT: 'Inhaled corticosteroids' — because inhaled steroids are controller medication, not a trigger.

questions.question: A 3-year-old presents with high fever, drooling, and tripod positioning. The nurse suspects epiglottitis. Which action is the priority?
options: Prepare for emergency airway management; do not inspect the throat
Immediately perform a throat culture
Place in supine position for comfort
Administer a nebulized bronchodilator
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Prepare for emergency airway management; do not inspect the throat' — because epiglottitis is a medical emergency — the swollen epiglottis can completely obstruct the airway. Never inspect the throat (may trigger spasm). Prepare for intubation. INCORRECT: 'Immediately perform a throat culture' — because unsafe. INCORRECT: 'Place in supine position for comfort' — because dangerous — upright position is preferred. INCORRECT: 'Administer a nebulized bronchodilator' — because ineffective for epiglottitis.

questions.question: Match each respiratory condition with its characteristic sign:
columns: Steatorrhea, chronic cough, failure to thrive
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Croup' matches 'Barking cough, stridor' — because croup. CORRECT: 'Epiglottitis' matches 'Drooling, tripod position' — because epiglottitis. CORRECT: 'Bronchiolitis (RSV)' matches 'Wheezing, crackles, nasal flaring' — because bronchiolitis. CORRECT: 'Cystic Fibrosis' matches 'Steatorrhea, chronic cough, failure to thrive' — because CF.

questions.question: A parent asks what the green zone on a peak flow meter means. Which response is correct?
options: Good asthma control — continue routine medications
Caution — use quick-relief inhaler and monitor closely
Medical emergency — seek immediate care
The child should stop all medications
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Good asthma control — continue routine medications' — because green zone (80-100% personal best) means good control — continue routine controller and rescue medications as prescribed. INCORRECT: 'Caution — use quick-relief inhaler and monitor closely' — because yellow zone. INCORRECT: 'Medical emergency — seek immediate care' — because red zone. INCORRECT: 'The child should stop all medications' — because dangerous — never stop medications without provider guidance.

questions.question: Parents call about their 18-month-old with mild croup (barking cough, no stridor at rest). Which home care instructions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Use a cool mist humidifier in the child's room
Have the child breathe cool night air
Give warm milk to soothe the throat
Position the child upright for sleep
Seek immediate care if stridor is present at rest
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Use a cool mist humidifier in the child's room' — because cool mist. CORRECT: 'Have the child breathe cool night air' — because cool air. CORRECT: 'Position the child upright for sleep' — because upright positioning. CORRECT: 'Seek immediate care if stridor is present at rest' — because seeking care for stridor at rest. INCORRECT: 'Give warm milk to soothe the throat' — because not harmful but not therapeutic.

questions.question: A parent asks when to give pancreatic enzymes to their child with cystic fibrosis. Which teaching is correct?
options: With every meal and snack to aid digestion
Only with breakfast and dinner
On an empty stomach 30 minutes before eating
Only when the child has abdominal pain
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'With every meal and snack to aid digestion' — because pancreatic enzymes must be given with all meals and snacks because the child's pancreas does not produce adequate enzymes for fat/protein digestion. Missing doses leads to malabsorption, steatorrhea, and poor growth. INCORRECT: 'Only with breakfast and dinner' — because need with all meals/snacks. INCORRECT: 'On an empty stomach 30 minutes before eating' — because with food. INCORRECT: 'Only when the child has abdominal pain' — because always with meals/snacks.

questions.question: Which intervention is most effective for preventing RSV bronchiolitis in high-risk infants?
options: Palivizumab (Synagis) monthly during RSV season
Yearly influenza vaccination only
Daily oral antibiotics
Avoiding all contact with other children
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Palivizumab (Synagis) monthly during RSV season' — because palivizumab (monoclonal antibody) is given monthly during RSV season (Nov-March) to high-risk infants (preterm, chronic lung disease, cardiac disease) to prevent severe RSV. INCORRECT: 'Yearly influenza vaccination only' — because for flu. INCORRECT: 'Daily oral antibiotics' — because not used for viral prevention. INCORRECT: 'Avoiding all contact with other children' — because impractical.

questions.question: A nurse is monitoring a child with respiratory distress. Which findings indicate worsening respiratory status requiring immediate intervention? (Select all that apply.)
options: Nasal flaring and grunting
Oxygen saturation of 92% on room air
Use of accessory muscles
Alert and interactive behavior
Cyanosis around lips and nail beds
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Nasal flaring and grunting' — because nasal flaring/grunting. CORRECT: 'Use of accessory muscles' — because accessory muscle use. CORRECT: 'Cyanosis around lips and nail beds' — because cyanosis. INCORRECT: 'Oxygen saturation of 92% on room air' — because borderline but not alone alarming. INCORRECT: 'Alert and interactive behavior' — because indicates compensation.

questions.question: A 3-month-old with a known VSD presents with tachypnea, diaphoresis during feeding, and poor weight gain. Which nursing intervention is the priority?
options: Provide small, frequent feedings with rest periods
Encourage the infant to finish entire bottle at each feeding
Place infant supine during and after feeds
Restrict fluid intake to prevent pulmonary edema
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Provide small, frequent feedings with rest periods' — because small, frequent feedings with rest periods reduce energy expenditure and prevent fatigue in heart failure. INCORRECT: 'Encourage the infant to finish entire bottle at each feeding' — because increases workload. INCORRECT: 'Place infant supine during and after feeds' — because upright position reduces work of breathing. INCORRECT: 'Restrict fluid intake to prevent pulmonary edema' — because adequate hydration is needed.

questions.question: A child with Kawasaki disease is admitted. Which findings are classic for this condition? (Select all that apply.)
options: Strawberry tongue and red cracked lips
Conjunctivitis without exudate
Peeling skin on palms and soles
High fever lasting more than 5 days
Bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Strawberry tongue and red cracked lips' — because strawberry tongue. CORRECT: 'Conjunctivitis without exudate' — because non-exudative conjunctivitis. CORRECT: 'Peeling skin on palms and soles' — because desquamation of palms/soles. CORRECT: 'High fever lasting more than 5 days' — because prolonged fever (>5 days). CORRECT: 'Bilateral cervical lymphadenopathy' — because cervical lymphadenopathy.

questions.question: A 2-year-old with tetralogy of Fallot has a hypercyanotic (tet) spell. Which intervention should the nurse implement first?
options: Place the child in knee-chest position
Administer oxygen via non-rebreather mask
Prepare for emergency cardiac surgery
Administer IV fluid bolus
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Place the child in knee-chest position' — because knee-chest position increases systemic vascular resistance, which reduces right-to-left shunting and improves oxygenation. This is the immediate first intervention. INCORRECT: 'Administer oxygen via non-rebreather mask' — because O2 is next. INCORRECT: 'Prepare for emergency cardiac surgery' — because not immediate. INCORRECT: 'Administer IV fluid bolus' — because may help but position is first.

questions.question: A child returns from cardiac catheterization. Which assessment finding requires immediate notification of the provider?
options: Diminished or absent pedal pulses in the catheterized leg
Mild swelling and bruising at the insertion site
Small amount of clear drainage on the dressing
Complaints of mild discomfort at the site
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Diminished or absent pedal pulses in the catheterized leg' — because diminished or absent pulses indicate possible arterial thrombosis or vessel occlusion — a complication requiring immediate intervention (anticoagulation, possible thrombectomy). INCORRECT: 'Mild swelling and bruising at the insertion site' — because expected. INCORRECT: 'Small amount of clear drainage on the dressing' — because minor. INCORRECT: 'Complaints of mild discomfort at the site' — because minor.

questions.question: A child with a history of strep throat now has joint pain, rash, and a heart murmur. Which interventions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Administer long-term penicillin prophylaxis
Monitor for signs of heart failure
Restrict activity during acute phase
Teach parents to seek care for sore throat promptly
Give aspirin for arthritis and fever
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer long-term penicillin prophylaxis' — because penicillin prevents recurrence and protects valves. CORRECT: 'Monitor for signs of heart failure' — because monitor for HF (carditis is major complication). CORRECT: 'Restrict activity during acute phase' — because rest during acute phase. CORRECT: 'Teach parents to seek care for sore throat promptly' — because teach prompt strep treatment. CORRECT: 'Give aspirin for arthritis and fever' — because aspirin for inflammation.

questions.question: A parent reports that their infant with a VSD sweats excessively during feeds and has poor weight gain. What does this indicate?
options: Heart failure — the infant is working hard to breathe and feed
Normal infant behavior — infants often sweat during feeds
Hyperthyroidism — the infant has increased metabolism
Infection — the infant is running a fever
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Heart failure — the infant is working hard to breathe and feed' — because excessive diaphoresis during feeds and poor weight gain in an infant with a VSD indicate heart failure — the infant is expending excessive energy on breathing and feeding due to pulmonary overcirculation. INCORRECT: 'Normal infant behavior — infants often sweat during feeds' — because not normal. INCORRECT: 'Hyperthyroidism — the infant has increased metabolism' — because not hyperthyroidism. INCORRECT: 'Infection — the infant is running a fever' — because not infection.

questions.question: A nurse is preparing to administer digoxin to a 6-month-old with heart failure. Which finding should prompt the nurse to withhold the dose?
options: Heart rate of 88 beats per minute
Serum potassium of 4.0 mEq/L
Respiratory rate of 32 breaths per minute
Weight gain of 2 ounces since yesterday
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Heart rate of 88 beats per minute' — because hold digoxin if HR <90-100 in infants (varies by age). HR 88 is below the threshold — giving digoxin could cause severe bradycardia. INCORRECT: 'Serum potassium of 4.0 mEq/L' — because K+ 4.0 is normal. INCORRECT: 'Respiratory rate of 32 breaths per minute' — because RR 32 is normal for infant. INCORRECT: 'Weight gain of 2 ounces since yesterday' — because 2 oz gain is expected.

questions.question: A nurse is providing discharge teaching for parents of a child with a repaired VSD. Which instructions are essential? (Select all that apply.)
options: Follow up with cardiology as scheduled
Report fever, weight loss, or signs of infection promptly
No activity restrictions are needed after healing
Complete antibiotic prophylaxis before dental procedures
Monitor for signs of heart failure (poor feeding, sweating, tachypnea)
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Follow up with cardiology as scheduled' — because cardiology follow-up. CORRECT: 'Report fever, weight loss, or signs of infection promptly' — because prompt infection reporting. CORRECT: 'Complete antibiotic prophylaxis before dental procedures' — because SBE prophylaxis before dental procedures. CORRECT: 'Monitor for signs of heart failure (poor feeding, sweating, tachypnea)' — because HF monitoring. INCORRECT: 'No activity restrictions are needed after healing' — because activity may be restricted initially or long-term depending on repair.

questions.question: A 4-year-old presents with fever, headache, neck stiffness, and photophobia. The nurse notes a positive Kernig sign. Which action is the priority?
options: Prepare for lumbar puncture and initiate droplet precautions
Administer oral acetaminophen for fever
Place in Trendelenburg position
Offer fluids to maintain hydration
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Prepare for lumbar puncture and initiate droplet precautions' — because meningitis (fever, nuchal rigidity, Kernig/Brudzinski signs, photophobia) is a medical emergency. Lumbar puncture for diagnosis and antibiotics are priority. Droplet precautions until 24 hours after antibiotics. INCORRECT: 'Administer oral acetaminophen for fever' — because secondary. INCORRECT: 'Place in Trendelenburg position' — because not priority. INCORRECT: 'Offer fluids to maintain hydration' — because not priority.

questions.question: A child with a history of seizures is admitted. Which precautions should be implemented? (Select all that apply.)
options: Pad bed side rails
Keep suction and oxygen at bedside
Restrain the child during a seizure
Place the child on their side during a seizure
Insert an oral airway during a seizure
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Pad bed side rails' — because padded rails. CORRECT: 'Keep suction and oxygen at bedside' — because suction/O2 at bedside. CORRECT: 'Place the child on their side during a seizure' — because side-lying position (prevents aspiration). INCORRECT: 'Restrain the child during a seizure' — because can cause injury. INCORRECT: 'Insert an oral airway during a seizure' — because dangerous — do not insert anything in mouth during seizure.

questions.question: A 3-month-old with hydrocephalus has which characteristic finding?
options: Increasing head circumference and bulging fontanelle
Small head size and closed fontanelle
Sunken eyes and dry fontanelle
High fever and rash
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Increasing head circumference and bulging fontanelle' — because hydrocephalus causes CSF accumulation → increased head circumference (crosses percentiles), bulging fontanelle, setting-sun sign, irritability. INCORRECT: 'Small head size and closed fontanelle' — because microcephaly. INCORRECT: 'Sunken eyes and dry fontanelle' — because dehydration. INCORRECT: 'High fever and rash' — because infection.

questions.question: A parent asks about giving aspirin to their child with a fever. Which teaching is correct?
options: Never give aspirin to children with viral illness — it is linked to Reye syndrome
Aspirin is the preferred antipyretic for all children
Aspirin is safe in children under 2 years
Aspirin can be given with acetaminophen for stronger effect
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Never give aspirin to children with viral illness — it is linked to Reye syndrome' — because aspirin is contraindicated in children with viral illness (flu, chickenpox) due to risk of Reye syndrome (acute encephalopathy + fatty liver). INCORRECT: 'Aspirin is the preferred antipyretic for all children' — because acetaminophen or ibuprofen are preferred. INCORRECT: 'Aspirin is safe in children under 2 years' — because not safe. INCORRECT: 'Aspirin can be given with acetaminophen for stronger effect' — because not recommended.

questions.question: Match each neurological condition with its key nursing intervention:
columns: Side-lying, suction at bedside, time seizure
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Meningitis' matches 'Droplet precautions, monitor ICP' — because meningitis. CORRECT: 'Seizures' matches 'Side-lying, suction at bedside, time seizure' — because seizures. CORRECT: 'Hydroceph with VP shunt' matches 'Monitor for shunt malfunction (headache, vomiting)' — because hydroceph/shunt. CORRECT: 'Cerebral palsy' matches 'Positioning, PT/OT, prevent contractures' — because CP.

questions.question: A 4-year-old with cerebral palsy presents with increased muscle tone, scissoring gait, and difficulty with fine motor tasks. Which intervention is most important?
options: Provide physical and occupational therapy to prevent contractures
Restrict all physical activity to prevent injury
Focus only on cognitive development — motor skills will improve naturally
Avoid stretching exercises — they cause pain
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Provide physical and occupational therapy to prevent contractures' — because CP is non-progressive but motor impairments persist. PT/OT maintain range of motion, prevent contractures, and maximize function. INCORRECT: 'Restrict all physical activity to prevent injury' — because activity is important. INCORRECT: 'Focus only on cognitive development — motor skills will improve naturally' — because motor skills need active intervention. INCORRECT: 'Avoid stretching exercises — they cause pain' — because stretching is part of therapy.

questions.question: A child with a VP shunt for hydroceph presents with headache, vomiting, and irritability. Which actions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Notify the provider immediately
Elevate the head of bed
Prepare for possible shunt revision
Give antiemetics and observe at home
Monitor fontanelle and head circumference
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Notify the provider immediately' — because shunt malfunction is an emergency. CORRECT: 'Elevate the head of bed' — because elevate HOB. CORRECT: 'Prepare for possible shunt revision' — because prepare for revision. CORRECT: 'Monitor fontanelle and head circumference' — because monitor fontanelle/HC. INCORRECT: 'Give antiemetics and observe at home' — because unsafe — these signs indicate increased ICP.

questions.question: A newborn with myelomeningocele is admitted. Which precaution is essential?
options: Latex precautions — high risk for latex allergy
Strict NPO status for 48 hours
Place infant supine only
Restrict fluid intake
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Latex precautions — high risk for latex allergy' — because children with spina bifida/myelomeningocele have high latex allergy risk due to frequent catheterizations, surgeries, and exposure. Use latex-free gloves, catheters, and supplies. INCORRECT: 'Strict NPO status for 48 hours' — because not needed. INCORRECT: 'Place infant supine only' — because not the precaution. INCORRECT: 'Restrict fluid intake' — because not needed.

questions.question: A child is recovering from bacterial meningitis. Which complications should parents be educated to monitor for? (Select all that apply.)
options: Hearing loss
Developmental delays
Seizures
Increased risk for repeat meningitis
Improved vision
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Hearing loss' — because hearing loss (most common). CORRECT: 'Developmental delays' — because developmental delays. CORRECT: 'Seizures' — because seizures. INCORRECT: 'Increased risk for repeat meningitis' — because not typical long-term outcome. INCORRECT: 'Improved vision' — because incorrect.

questions.question: A parent of a child on phenytoin for seizures asks about medication compliance. Which teaching is correct?
options: Do not stop medication abruptly — seizures may increase or status epilepticus may occur
It is safe to miss doses occasionally if the child feels fine
Double the dose if a dose is missed
The child can stop medication after 6 months seizure-free
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Do not stop medication abruptly — seizures may increase or status epilepticus may occur' — because abrupt discontinuation of antiepileptics can trigger breakthrough seizures or status epilepticus. Medication should only be tapered under provider guidance. INCORRECT: 'It is safe to miss doses occasionally if the child feels fine' — because unsafe. INCORRECT: 'Double the dose if a dose is missed' — because unsafe. INCORRECT: 'The child can stop medication after 6 months seizure-free' — because unsafe.

questions.question: A 6-year-old with sickle cell disease is in vaso-occlusive crisis. Which intervention is the priority?
options: Administer IV fluids and pain medication
Restrict fluids to prevent pulmonary edema
Apply ice packs to painful joints
Encourage ambulation to reduce stiffness
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer IV fluids and pain medication' — because IV hydration maintains blood flow and prevents further sickling; pain management (opioids + NSAIDs) is essential. INCORRECT: 'Restrict fluids to prevent pulmonary edema' — because hydration is critical. INCORRECT: 'Apply ice packs to painful joints' — because ice can worsen vasoconstriction. INCORRECT: 'Encourage ambulation to reduce stiffness' — because unsafe during crisis.

questions.question: A child with hemophilia A has a joint bleed (hemarthrosis) in the knee. Which interventions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Administer factor VIII replacement immediately
Apply ice and elevate the joint
Encourage passive range of motion
Avoid NSAIDs and aspirin
Immobilize the joint
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer factor VIII replacement immediately' — because factor replacement is priority for acute bleed. CORRECT: 'Apply ice and elevate the joint' — because ice and elevation are appropriate (RICE). CORRECT: 'Avoid NSAIDs and aspirin' — because NSAIDs/aspirin are avoided (interfere with platelet function). CORRECT: 'Immobilize the joint' — because immobilize during acute phase. INCORRECT: 'Encourage passive range of motion' — because avoided during acute bleed.

questions.question: A child undergoing chemotherapy for leukemia has an ANC of 400. Which precaution is most important?
options: Neutropenic precautions — hand hygiene, avoid crowds, no fresh flowers
Bleeding precautions only — monitor for petechiae
Contact precautions — gown and gloves
Droplet precautions — mask and eye protection
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Neutropenic precautions — hand hygiene, avoid crowds, no fresh flowers' — because ANC <500 indicates severe neutropenia — infection risk is critical. Neutropenic precautions include strict hand hygiene, avoiding crowds/fresh flowers/raw foods, and monitoring for fever. INCORRECT: 'Bleeding precautions only — monitor for petechiae' — because secondary. INCORRECT: 'Contact precautions — gown and gloves' — because for other conditions. INCORRECT: 'Droplet precautions — mask and eye protection' — because for other conditions.

questions.question: A 2-year-old is diagnosed with iron-deficiency anemia. Which teaching is correct?
options: Give iron supplement with vitamin C (orange juice) on an empty stomach or between meals
Give iron with milk to improve absorption
Iron supplements cause diarrhea — stop if this occurs
No dietary changes are needed with supplements
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Give iron supplement with vitamin C (orange juice) on an empty stomach or between meals' — because iron is best absorbed on empty stomach with vitamin C (enhances absorption). Avoid milk/calcium with iron (inhibits absorption). INCORRECT: 'Give iron with milk to improve absorption' — because inhibits absorption. INCORRECT: 'Iron supplements cause diarrhea — stop if this occurs' — because causes constipation, not diarrhea. INCORRECT: 'No dietary changes are needed with supplements' — because iron-rich foods are important.

questions.question: Parents of a child with leukemia ask about home care. Which instructions should be included? (Select all that apply.)
options: Wash hands frequently and avoid sick contacts
Monitor for fever and report immediately
Avoid fresh fruits and vegetables
Encourage the child to attend school normally
Report bleeding, bruising, or petechiae promptly
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Wash hands frequently and avoid sick contacts' — because hand hygiene and avoiding sick contacts prevent infection. CORRECT: 'Monitor for fever and report immediately' — because fever is an emergency in neutropenia. CORRECT: 'Avoid fresh fruits and vegetables' — because fresh produce can carry pathogens (avoid during neutropenia). CORRECT: 'Report bleeding, bruising, or petechiae promptly' — because monitor for bleeding (thrombocytopenia). INCORRECT: 'Encourage the child to attend school normally' — because school exposes to illness; home schooling or limited contact may be needed.

questions.question: A child is suspected of having Wilms tumor. Which action is contraindicated?
options: Palpating the abdomen to assess the tumor
Obtaining a CT scan of the abdomen
Monitoring blood pressure
Administering preoperative chemotherapy
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Palpating the abdomen to assess the tumor' — because never palpate the abdomen in Wilms tumor — it can cause tumor rupture and spread. Avoid rough handling. INCORRECT: 'Obtaining a CT scan of the abdomen' — because diagnosis is by imaging. INCORRECT: 'Monitoring blood pressure' — because needed. INCORRECT: 'Administering preoperative chemotherapy' — because needed.

questions.question: A toddler with iron-deficiency anemia is pale, fatigued, and irritable. The nurse notes tachycardia. Which finding indicates the anemia is improving?
options: Increased energy and improved appetite
Increased heart rate
Pallor becoming more pronounced
Decreased hemoglobin level
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Increased energy and improved appetite' — because improved energy and appetite indicate treatment response. INCORRECT: 'Increased heart rate' — because tachycardia is a sign of anemia. INCORRECT: 'Pallor becoming more pronounced' — because deterioration. INCORRECT: 'Decreased hemoglobin level' — because worsening.

questions.question: A child with leukemia presents with petechiae and epistaxis. Which lab values would the nurse expect? (Select all that apply.)
options: Low platelet count
Low hemoglobin and hematocrit
Elevated white blood cell count
Prolonged PT/PTT
Low ANC
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Low platelet count' — because low platelets (bleeding). CORRECT: 'Low hemoglobin and hematocrit' — because low Hgb/Hct (anemia). CORRECT: 'Low ANC' — because low ANC (neutropenia). INCORRECT: 'Elevated white blood cell count' — because variable — WBC may be high (leukemia cells) or low (suppression). INCORRECT: 'Prolonged PT/PTT' — because for coagulation disorders.

questions.question: A 3-year-old is diagnosed with lead poisoning (blood lead level 65 µg/dL). Which intervention is priority?
options: Initiate chelation therapy (succimer or EDTA)
Provide nutritional education only
Monitor and recheck in 6 months
Give iron supplements to treat anemia
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Initiate chelation therapy (succimer or EDTA)' — because BLL >45 µg/dL requires chelation (succimer oral or EDTA IV/IM). This removes lead from tissues. INCORRECT: 'Provide nutritional education only' — because alone is insufficient. INCORRECT: 'Monitor and recheck in 6 months' — because for lower levels. INCORRECT: 'Give iron supplements to treat anemia' — because treats anemia but not lead poisoning.

questions.question: A nurse is teaching a family about preventing sickle cell crisis. Which measures should be included? (Select all that apply.)
options: Maintain adequate hydration at all times
Avoid extreme temperatures (hot or cold)
Promptly treat infections with antibiotics
Encourage contact sports for cardiovascular health
Ensure yearly influenza vaccination
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Maintain adequate hydration at all times' — because hydration prevents sickling. CORRECT: 'Avoid extreme temperatures (hot or cold)' — because extreme temps cause vasoconstriction. CORRECT: 'Promptly treat infections with antibiotics' — because infections trigger crisis (treat promptly). CORRECT: 'Ensure yearly influenza vaccination' — because flu vaccine prevents respiratory infections. INCORRECT: 'Encourage contact sports for cardiovascular health' — because contact sports can cause trauma and sickling.

questions.question: Parents of an infant in a Pavlik harness for DDH ask about care. Which statement is correct?
options: The harness should be worn 23–24 hours per day as prescribed
Parents should adjust the straps if they seem too tight
Remove the harness at night for comfortable sleep
It is safe to place the infant prone in the harness
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'The harness should be worn 23–24 hours per day as prescribed' — because Pavlik harness is worn 23-24 hours/day (may remove briefly for bathing per provider). INCORRECT: 'Parents should adjust the straps if they seem too tight' — because parents should NOT adjust straps — provider adjusts. INCORRECT: 'Remove the harness at night for comfortable sleep' — because should not remove. INCORRECT: 'It is safe to place the infant prone in the harness' — because prone is unsafe.

questions.question: A child with a fractured forearm has a cast. Which findings indicate possible compartment syndrome? (Select all that apply.)
options: Severe pain not relieved by medication
Pallor and coolness of the fingers
Numbness and tingling in the hand
Capillary refill of 2 seconds
Inability to move fingers
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Severe pain not relieved by medication' — because severe pain. CORRECT: 'Pallor and coolness of the fingers' — because pallor/coolness. CORRECT: 'Numbness and tingling in the hand' — because paresthesia (numbness/tingling). CORRECT: 'Inability to move fingers' — because paralysis (inability to move). INCORRECT: 'Capillary refill of 2 seconds' — because CR 2 sec is normal.

questions.question: A nurse is screening an adolescent for scoliosis. Which finding is most concerning?
options: Uneven shoulders and a rib hump visible on forward bend test
Mild back pain after sports
Height increase of 2 inches in 6 months
Slight asymmetry of the waist
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Uneven shoulders and a rib hump visible on forward bend test' — because uneven shoulders and rib hump (Adams forward bend test) indicate significant spinal curvature requiring evaluation. INCORRECT: 'Mild back pain after sports' — because normal. INCORRECT: 'Height increase of 2 inches in 6 months' — because normal. INCORRECT: 'Slight asymmetry of the waist' — because may be observed.

questions.question: A child with juvenile idiopathic arthritis (JIA) has morning stiffness and swollen knees. Which intervention is most helpful?
options: Apply warm compresses or take a warm bath in the morning
Apply ice packs to the joints
Restrict all physical activity
Administer pain medication only at bedtime
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Apply warm compresses or take a warm bath in the morning' — because warm compresses/bath reduces morning stiffness in JIA. INCORRECT: 'Apply ice packs to the joints' — because ice is for acute injury. INCORRECT: 'Restrict all physical activity' — because activity (with PT) is important. INCORRECT: 'Administer pain medication only at bedtime' — because pain medication timing should be before activity.

questions.question: A child is in Bryant's traction for a femur fracture. Which nursing interventions are essential? (Select all that apply.)
options: Ensure weights hang freely and off the floor
Perform frequent neurovascular checks
Elevate the head of bed to 45 degrees
Remove weights briefly for repositioning
Monitor for skin breakdown under straps
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Ensure weights hang freely and off the floor' — because weights must hang freely (countertraction). CORRECT: 'Perform frequent neurovascular checks' — because frequent neurovascular checks (5 P's). CORRECT: 'Monitor for skin breakdown under straps' — because skin monitoring. INCORRECT: 'Elevate the head of bed to 45 degrees' — because HOB should be flat or minimal elevation for traction. INCORRECT: 'Remove weights briefly for repositioning' — because unsafe — never remove weights without provider order.

questions.question: A newborn is diagnosed with clubfoot. Which treatment approach is the gold standard?
options: Ponseti method — serial casting followed by bracing
Immediate surgical release
Observation — most cases resolve spontaneously
Physical therapy alone
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Ponseti method — serial casting followed by bracing' — because Ponseti method (serial manipulation and casting starting at birth, followed by abduction bracing) is the gold standard for clubfoot. INCORRECT: 'Immediate surgical release' — because surgery is for cases that don't respond. INCORRECT: 'Observation — most cases resolve spontaneously' — because treatment is urgent. INCORRECT: 'Physical therapy alone' — because not sufficient.

questions.question: A 7-year-old with osteomyelitis is on IV antibiotics. Which finding indicates the infection is improving?
options: Decreased pain and ability to bear weight
Increased localized swelling and redness
Persistent fever and chills
Elevated ESR and WBC
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Decreased pain and ability to bear weight' — because decreased pain and improved mobility indicate response to antibiotics. INCORRECT: 'Increased localized swelling and redness' — because indicate worsening infection. INCORRECT: 'Persistent fever and chills' — because indicate worsening infection. INCORRECT: 'Elevated ESR and WBC' — because indicate worsening infection.

questions.question: A parent is caring for a child with a new cast. Which instructions are essential? (Select all that apply.)
options: Keep the cast dry — cover for bathing
Elevate the extremity for the first 24–48 hours
Insert objects into the cast to scratch itch
Monitor for increased pain, swelling, or numbness
Petal the edges if they become rough
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Keep the cast dry — cover for bathing' — because keep cast dry. CORRECT: 'Elevate the extremity for the first 24–48 hours' — because elevate to reduce swelling. CORRECT: 'Monitor for increased pain, swelling, or numbness' — because monitor for compartment syndrome signs (pain, swelling, numbness). CORRECT: 'Petal the edges if they become rough' — because petal rough edges. INCORRECT: 'Insert objects into the cast to scratch itch' — because dangerous — can cause skin breakdown.

questions.question: A child with celiac disease is being discharged. Which dietary instruction is correct?
options: Avoid all foods containing wheat, rye, and barley — read labels carefully
A gluten-free diet is only needed during symptom flare-ups
Oats are always safe for children with celiac disease
Rice and corn products should also be avoided
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Avoid all foods containing wheat, rye, and barley — read labels carefully' — because celiac disease requires strict lifelong gluten-free diet — wheat, rye, barley, and contaminated oats must be avoided. Labels must be checked. INCORRECT: 'A gluten-free diet is only needed during symptom flare-ups' — because diet is lifelong. INCORRECT: 'Oats are always safe for children with celiac disease' — because oats are often contaminated. INCORRECT: 'Rice and corn products should also be avoided' — because rice and corn are gluten-free.

questions.question: A child with nephrotic syndrome has significant edema and low albumin. Which dietary restriction is most important?
options: Sodium restriction to reduce fluid retention
Protein restriction to protect the kidneys
Carbohydrate restriction to prevent hyperglycemia
No dietary restrictions are needed
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Sodium restriction to reduce fluid retention' — because sodium restriction reduces fluid retention and edema. Protein is actually needed (losses in urine) — not restricted. INCORRECT: 'Protein restriction to protect the kidneys' — because protein is actually needed. INCORRECT: 'Carbohydrate restriction to prevent hyperglycemia' — because not needed. INCORRECT: 'No dietary restrictions are needed' — because incorrect.

questions.question: Parents ask about home management for mild diarrhea in their 18-month-old. Which instructions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Continue breastfeeding or formula as usual
Offer oral rehydration solution in small frequent amounts
Give juice and sports drinks to replace fluids
Return to regular diet as tolerated
Seek care if vomiting persists or signs of dehydration appear
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Continue breastfeeding or formula as usual' — because continue milk/breastfeeding. CORRECT: 'Offer oral rehydration solution in small frequent amounts' — because use ORS in small amounts. CORRECT: 'Return to regular diet as tolerated' — because return to regular diet. CORRECT: 'Seek care if vomiting persists or signs of dehydration appear' — because seek care for red flags. INCORRECT: 'Give juice and sports drinks to replace fluids' — because juice/sports drinks can worsen diarrhea (high sugar).

questions.question: A 9-year-old presents with abdominal pain, nausea, and vomiting. The pain started around the umbilicus and is now in the RLQ. Which finding is most concerning?
options: Sudden relief of pain with abdominal rigidity
Mild RLQ tenderness
Low-grade fever of 100.4°F
Nausea without vomiting
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Sudden relief of pain with abdominal rigidity' — because sudden pain relief with rigidity suggests perforation — an emergency requiring immediate surgery. INCORRECT: 'Mild RLQ tenderness' — because expected. INCORRECT: 'Low-grade fever of 100.4°F' — because common. INCORRECT: 'Nausea without vomiting' — because early.

questions.question: A child with type 1 diabetes has a blood glucose of 52 mg/dL and is shaky and sweaty. Which action is correct?
options: Give 15 grams of fast-acting carbohydrate and recheck in 15 minutes
Administer insulin immediately
Have the child exercise to lower glucose further
Send to ED — this is a medical emergency
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Give 15 grams of fast-acting carbohydrate and recheck in 15 minutes' — because hypoglycemia (<70 mg/dL) is treated with 15g fast-acting carb (juice, glucose tabs), recheck in 15 min. If unconscious or <50, use glucagon. INCORRECT: 'Administer insulin immediately' — because would worsen it. INCORRECT: 'Have the child exercise to lower glucose further' — because would worsen it. INCORRECT: 'Send to ED — this is a medical emergency' — because for severe/unresponsive.

questions.question: A child with acute poststreptococcal glomerulonephritis (APSGN) is admitted. Which findings are expected? (Select all that apply.)
options: Hematuria and proteinuria
Hypertension
Periorbital and generalized edema
Increased urine output
History of recent strep throat
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Hematuria and proteinuria' — because hematuria and proteinuria are classic findings. CORRECT: 'Hypertension' — because hypertension is expected. CORRECT: 'Periorbital and generalized edema' — because edema is expected. CORRECT: 'History of recent strep throat' — because APSGN occurs 1-3 weeks after strep infection. INCORRECT: 'Increased urine output' — because decreased urine output (oliguria) is expected.

questions.question: A parent calls about their child with type 1 diabetes who has a cold. Which advice is correct?
options: Check blood glucose more frequently and never skip insulin even if not eating
Skip insulin doses until the child feels better
Give extra insulin to cover the illness
Stop insulin and monitor glucose only
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Check blood glucose more frequently and never skip insulin even if not eating' — because sick days require frequent glucose checks and insulin should NEVER be skipped — illness can cause hyperglycemia. May need extra insulin. INCORRECT: 'Skip insulin doses until the child feels better' — because insulin should never be skipped. INCORRECT: 'Give extra insulin to cover the illness' — because extra insulin can cause hypoglycemia; may need adjustment but not automatic extra. INCORRECT: 'Stop insulin and monitor glucose only' — because stopping insulin is dangerous.

questions.question: A child with type 1 diabetes presents with polyuria, polydipsia, vomiting, and fruity breath. Which action is the priority?
options: Initiate IV fluid resuscitation and insulin infusion
Give oral glucose tablets
Administer antiemetic medication
Send home with sick-day instructions
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Initiate IV fluid resuscitation and insulin infusion' — because fruity breath + polyuria/polydipsia + vomiting = DKA — a medical emergency. IV fluids (rehydration) and insulin infusion are priority. INCORRECT: 'Give oral glucose tablets' — because glucose is for hypoglycemia, not DKA. INCORRECT: 'Administer antiemetic medication' — because treats vomiting but does not address DKA. INCORRECT: 'Send home with sick-day instructions' — because DKA requires immediate hospitalization.

questions.question: Match each child with the appropriate pain assessment tool:
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'A 4-month-old postoperative infant' matches 'FLACC Scale' — because infants are nonverbal; FLACC assesses face, legs, activity, cry, consolability. CORRECT: 'A 3-year-old with a fractured arm' matches 'FACES Scale' — because preschoolers understand faces. CORRECT: 'A 9-year-old with abdominal pain' matches 'Numeric 0–10 Scale' — because school-age can use numeric. CORRECT: 'A 14-year-old with a sprained ankle' matches 'Oucher or Numeric Scale' — because adolescents use numeric or Oucher.

questions.question: A child presents with wheezing, cough, and dyspnea. Identify the primary concern and appropriate intervention.
options: Asthma exacerbation
Bronchiolitis
Epiglottitis
Administer albuterol nebulizer
Prepare for intubation
Administer racemic epinephrine
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Asthma exacerbation' — because wheezing, cough, dyspnea are classic asthma signs. CORRECT: 'Administer albuterol nebulizer' — because albuterol is the first-line bronchodilator for acute asthma. INCORRECT: 'Bronchiolitis' — because bronchiolitis (RSV) presents with crackles, nasal flaring in infants. INCORRECT: 'Epiglottitis' — because epiglottitis presents with drooling, tripod positioning. INCORRECT: 'Prepare for intubation' — because for severe airway compromise, not first-line. INCORRECT: 'Administer racemic epinephrine' — because for croup, not asthma.

questions.question: A dehydrated infant presents with _____ fontanelle and _____ mucous membranes. The priority intervention is _____ _____ fluids.
options: sunken
dry
IV
oral rehydration
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'sunken' — because sunken fontanelle indicates dehydration. CORRECT: 'dry' — because dry mucous membranes indicate dehydration. CORRECT: 'IV' — because IV fluids are priority for moderate-severe dehydration. INCORRECT: 'oral rehydration' — because oral is for mild dehydration; IV for moderate-severe.

questions.question: Select the statement that indicates correct understanding of toddler safety: 'I will keep all medications locked up and turn pot handles to the back of the stove.'
options: I will keep all medications locked up and turn pot handles to the back of the stove.
I will let my toddler play near the pool unsupervised.
I will place my toddler in a forward-facing car seat.
I will give my toddler honey for cough.
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'I will keep all medications locked up and turn pot handles to the back of the stove.' — because locked medications (poison prevention) and pot handles back (burn prevention) are correct toddler safety. INCORRECT: 'I will let my toddler play near the pool unsupervised.' — because unsafe (drowning risk). INCORRECT: 'I will place my toddler in a forward-facing car seat.' — because incorrect (rear-facing until age 2). INCORRECT: 'I will give my toddler honey for cough.' — because unsafe (botulism risk in children under 1).

questions.question: A child with sickle cell disease presents with severe pain, fever, and tachypnea. Which interventions are priority? (Select all that apply.)
options: Administer IV fluids and oxygen
Administer pain medication promptly
Apply ice to painful joints
Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory status
Encourage ambulation
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer IV fluids and oxygen' — because IV hydration maintains blood flow and prevents further sickling; oxygen prevents hypoxemia. CORRECT: 'Administer pain medication promptly' — because pain management is essential. CORRECT: 'Monitor oxygen saturation and respiratory status' — because respiratory monitoring is critical. INCORRECT: 'Apply ice to painful joints' — because ice worsens vasoconstriction. INCORRECT: 'Encourage ambulation' — because unsafe during crisis.

questions.question: Order the steps for treating a child with type 1 diabetes experiencing mild hypoglycemia:
options: Recheck blood glucose in 15 minutes
Give 15 grams of fast-acting carbohydrate
Recognize signs of shakiness and sweating
If glucose still low, repeat carbohydrate and notify provider
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Recognize signs of shakiness and sweating' — because first step is recognizing signs of hypoglycemia. CORRECT: 'Give 15 grams of fast-acting carbohydrate' — because treat with 15g fast-acting carb (juice, glucose tabs). CORRECT: 'Recheck blood glucose in 15 minutes' — because recheck 15 min after treatment. CORRECT: 'If glucose still low, repeat carbohydrate and notify provider' — because if still low, repeat and notify.

questions.question: A nurse has four pediatric patients. Which should be seen first?
options: A child with leukemia, ANC 200, and temperature 102°F
A child with asthma in green zone, requesting albuterol
A child with DDH in Pavlik harness, parents requesting education
A child with sickle cell, pain score 4/10, requesting pain medication
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'A child with leukemia, ANC 200, and temperature 102°F' — because neutropenia + fever = neutropenic fever — a medical emergency requiring immediate antibiotics. INCORRECT: 'A child with asthma in green zone, requesting albuterol' — because stable asthma in green zone. INCORRECT: 'A child with DDH in Pavlik harness, parents requesting education' — because non-urgent education request. INCORRECT: 'A child with sickle cell, pain score 4/10, requesting pain medication' — because stable pain level 4/10.

questions.question: A child with cystic fibrosis is admitted with respiratory distress. Which interventions are appropriate? (Select all that apply.)
options: Administer dornase alfa nebulizer
Perform chest physiotherapy
Restrict fluids
Provide high-calorie diet
Monitor SpO2 and respiratory status
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer dornase alfa nebulizer' — because dornase alfa thins mucus. CORRECT: 'Perform chest physiotherapy' — because CPT clears secretions. CORRECT: 'Provide high-calorie diet' — because CF requires high-calorie nutrition. CORRECT: 'Monitor SpO2 and respiratory status' — because respiratory monitoring is critical. INCORRECT: 'Restrict fluids' — because hydration thins mucus and is important.

questions.question: Using the Pediatric Assessment Triangle, which child requires emergent triage?
options: An infant who is limp, has grunting respirations, and mottled skin
A toddler crying with a scraped knee
A school-age child with a cough and fever of 101°F
An adolescent with a sprained ankle
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'An infant who is limp, has grunting respirations, and mottled skin' — because limp tone (appearance abnormal), grunting (breathing abnormal), mottled skin (circulation abnormal) = all three PAT components abnormal → emergent. INCORRECT: 'A toddler crying with a scraped knee' — because minor injury. INCORRECT: 'A school-age child with a cough and fever of 101°F' — because stable. INCORRECT: 'An adolescent with a sprained ankle' — because non-urgent.

questions.question: A 6-year-old is crying and rates pain as 8/10 before a dressing change. Which action is most appropriate?
options: Administer prescribed analgesic 30–60 minutes before the procedure
Proceed with the dressing change without medication
Use distraction only
Tell the child it won't hurt
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer prescribed analgesic 30–60 minutes before the procedure' — because premedicate 30–60 min before painful procedures. INCORRECT: 'Proceed with the dressing change without medication' — because inadequate pain management. INCORRECT: 'Use distraction only' — because distraction alone is insufficient for severe pain 8/10. INCORRECT: 'Tell the child it won't hurt' — because dishonest and inappropriate.

questions.question: A child presents with respiratory distress. Which findings indicate immediate intervention is needed? (Select all that apply.)
options: Nasal flaring and grunting
Cyanosis
SpO2 95% on room air
Use of accessory muscles
Alert and talking in full sentences
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Nasal flaring and grunting' — because nasal flaring and grunting indicate severe respiratory distress. CORRECT: 'Cyanosis' — because cyanosis is a sign of severe hypoxemia. CORRECT: 'Use of accessory muscles' — because accessory muscle use indicates respiratory distress. INCORRECT: 'SpO2 95% on room air' — because borderline; monitor closely. INCORRECT: 'Alert and talking in full sentences' — because indicates compensation; still concerning but less emergent.

questions.question: Which teaching indicates correct understanding of infant safety?
options: I will place my baby on the back to sleep and use a firm mattress
I will place my baby on the stomach to prevent choking
I will co-sleep with my baby for bonding
I will give my baby honey for constipation
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'I will place my baby on the back to sleep and use a firm mattress' — because back to sleep + firm mattress = Safe Sleep (SIDS prevention). INCORRECT: 'I will place my baby on the stomach to prevent choking' — because prone position increases SIDS risk. INCORRECT: 'I will co-sleep with my baby for bonding' — because co-sleeping increases SIDS risk per AAP. INCORRECT: 'I will give my baby honey for constipation' — because honey is unsafe under age 1 (botulism risk).

questions.question: A child with suspected meningitis has photophobia and neck stiffness. Which interventions are priority? (Select all that apply.)
options: Initiate droplet precautions
Prepare for lumbar puncture
Monitor for seizures and increased ICP
Administer antibiotics after cultures
Place in Trendelenburg position
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Initiate droplet precautions' — because meningitis is droplet precaution. CORRECT: 'Prepare for lumbar puncture' — because LP confirms diagnosis. CORRECT: 'Monitor for seizures and increased ICP' — because monitor for seizures and ICP. CORRECT: 'Administer antibiotics after cultures' — because antibiotics after cultures. INCORRECT: 'Place in Trendelenburg position' — because unsafe; Trendelenburg can worsen ICP.

questions.question: A child with leukemia and ANC of 300 presents with fever of 103°F. Which action is most urgent?
options: Administer broad-spectrum antibiotics immediately
Give antipyretics and monitor
Draw blood cultures and wait for results
Send home with fever instructions
questions.rationale: CORRECT: 'Administer broad-spectrum antibiotics immediately' — because neutropenic fever is an emergency — antibiotics must be given immediately (within 1 hour). INCORRECT: 'Give antipyretics and monitor' — because antipyretics mask fever but don't treat the underlying infection. INCORRECT: 'Draw blood cultures and wait for results' — because cultures should be drawn but should not delay antibiotics. INCORRECT: 'Send home with fever instructions' — because neutropenic fever requires immediate hospitalization.
